patches.find: the same post-hoc pattern seen across the model (see Section 2b.3).
patches.replace:
the same post-hoc pattern seen across the model (see Section 2b.3).

The reconciliation requires formula-switching. Tracing the derivation chain exposes a deeper problem. The 10.59 Hz 'raw theoretical' comes from Schumann's 1952 globe formula: f1 = c√2/(2πR) where R = 6,371 km — a formula for circumferential modes in a spherical shell. WIN-029 then inverts the quarter-wave formula f = c/(4H) to recover H = 9,572 km — a formula for vertical standing waves in a linear waveguide. The dome's 'reconciliation' requires these two formulas to describe the same cavity, but they describe geometrically incompatible systems. Using either formula consistently breaks the chain: the quarter-wave formula applied to the dome's own H₀ = 8,537 km gives 8.78 Hz (not 10.59), requiring only 11% damping instead of 26%; the globe formula inverted on 7.83 Hz gives R = 8,616 km — a spherical cavity radius, not a dome height. The 26% figure requires the globe's number; the 9,572 km requires a different formula. No single physical model produces both.

The Schumann–Tesla 'correlation' is circular. The dome's Live Power Dashboard claims a Schumann ↔ Tesla Frequency correlation of r = 0.999, described as 'same aetheric circuit, H₀ = 8537 km.' But Domain #1 (Schumann) hardcodes predicted=7.83, observed=7.83, error=0.0%, and Domain #2 (Tesla) derives f = va/(2 × r_disc) using locked constants. Two static, hardcoded values trivially correlate at r ≈ 1.000 because neither varies. This cross-domain 'correlation' is a comparison of two constants, not a discovery about physical coupling. The dome's codebase confirms the vacuity: no cavity geometry calculation, no damping model, no derivation chain exists for the Schumann domain. The 'reconciliation' exists only as prose on the evolution page.
patches.rationale: EXP-029 integration: append formula-switching argument and Schumann-Tesla r=0.999 debunk. The dome's reconciliation uses geometrically incompatible formulas (spherical shell for 10.59 Hz vs quarter-wave linear for 9,572 km). The r=0.999 correlation is between two hardcoded constants.

patches.find: Even the sole plausible match — Polaris at r ≈ 5,960 km — is off: H(5960) = 8537·exp(−5960/8619) = 4,274 km versus the claimed 4,750 km, a 10% miss. The V12 'key discovery' fails to reconcile any of the three values it was introduced to reconcile.
patches.replace: Even the sole plausible match — Polaris at r ≈ 5,960 km — is internally inconsistent within the dome's own pages. The model page states H at Chapel Hill (r ≈ 5,960 km) is '~4,359 km,' yet the Polaris claim uses 4,750 km. Computing directly: H(5960) = 8537·exp(−5960/8619) = 4,274 km — close to the 4,359 km figure but 10% below the 4,750 km claim. The dome cannot agree with itself on the height at a single location. The V12 'key discovery' fails to reconcile any of the three values it was introduced to reconcile.
patches.rationale: EXP-029 integration: add dome's own internal inconsistency — model page says H(5960)=4,359 km but Polaris claim uses 4,750 km. Dome cannot agree with itself on height at a single location.

patches.find: no solid conductive ceiling is compatible with the measured quality factor.
patches.replace: no solid conductive ceiling is compatible with the measured quality factor. The dome's evolution page explicitly claims WIN-002 and WIN-029 are 'complementary, not contradictory' — that aetheric damping (WIN-002) shifts the frequency, and the shifted frequency back-derives the dome height (WIN-029). This reconciliation fails: it requires switching between two incompatible formulas (Schumann's spherical-shell formula for 10.59 Hz, a linear quarter-wave formula for 9,572 km), and no consistent damping ratio links the dome's claimed theoretical frequency, measured frequency, and geometric height. See WIN-002 for the full analysis.
patches.rationale: EXP-029 integration: append cross-reference to WIN-002 for the formula-switching reconciliation analysis. Connects WIN-029 readers to the full rebuttal.

WIN-058 Cycle 2 curmudgeon: code_analysis.derives_from_dome must be false. Our own detail_evidence states 'The identity θ = −lonE is not derived from dome geometry' — it follows from the definition of geographic longitude. Tag was set to true during Cycle 1 when reviewing the 0.9941 factor, but that claim is no longer the focus of WIN-058.

patches.rationale: WIN-058 Cycle 2 curmudgeon: finding says '700×' but detail_evidence says '600–700×'. Use the range consistently.

patches.rationale: WIN-058 Cycle 2 curmudgeon: V12's solar-noon-UTC proxy attribution is wrong. The evolution page states 'V9 theta bug fixed' under V12, meaning V9 had the solar-noon-UTC proxy. V12 introduced the 0.9941 partial fix. V13 introduced θ = −lonE.

patches.rationale: WIN-058 Cycle 2 curmudgeon: 'the V12 formula used solar noon UTC' is incorrect. V9 had the solar-noon proxy; V12 introduced the 0.9941 scale as a partial fix. Correct the version attribution.

patches.rationale: WIN-058 Cycle 2 curmudgeon: kernel of truth says 'real bug in its own V12 coordinate system' but the bug was in V9; V12 partially fixed it. Correct attribution.
